Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

I'm not against this patch, but "lustre_fid" needs to be added to the list of deprecated symbols that checkpatch.pl looks at, so that we don't get new patches that are using it.

It looks like it is only used in the HSM API, and doesn't change the ABI, but it could cause copytools to fail building?

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Since the struct vs. typedef issue is a kernel style thing, what about moving the lustre_fid typedef into lustreapi.h, so it isn't in the kernel, but doesn't change the userspace library API?
Uploaded patch set 3.

(9 comments)

Patch Set 3:

The c++ mangling can happen for lots of things. That can be resolved with extern "C". If I add extern "C" to the headers in this patch would you okay with using struct lustre_fid instead of lustre_fid or are you set to keeping lustre_fid forever?

Patch Set 3:

I think the "extern C" with the #define to ensure existing programs using lustre_fid continue to build on both old and new libraries would be enough. The only other consideration is userspace bs. Kenrelspace structures, but since lu_fid is widely used for various data structures and interfaces already, I don't think that is a valid abstraction anymore.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

I just added extern "C" to only the headers impacted by this patch. Once the typedef cleanup for lnet lands I will push a patch that adds extern "C" to every header exposed to user land.

Patch Set 4:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

A lot more changed between patches 3 and 4 than just adding extern C

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Ben, if you are comparing patch 5 vs. 4 you will see all of the changes from different patches that have landed to master that modified those files since patch #4 was generated.  This is unfortunately how Gerrit works, since it is only using "git diff <filename>" to generate the difference, and doesn't e.g. do a diff-of-diffs to see only what has changed in the two patches.

If there are a lot of unrelated patches landed between rebases, it is often better to just look at the new patch, and open a separate tab/window with the two patch versions and do visual comparison of them.

James, I think just one minor change is needed.

parents.subject: LU-10220 mdd: fix buf alloc in mdd_changelog_data_store_by_fid

commit.subject: LU-6142 uapi: Get rid of lustre_fid typedef
commit.message:
LU-6142 uapi: Get rid of lustre_fid typedef

Replace it with struct lu_fid. Update the userland code and man
pages to reflect this change.

Linux-commit: d8f6bc9a53f97d1ea4b2b955672904338643308b

Test-Parameters: trivial

Change-Id: I0b7e0770dd9da9bdac55c02c2ec98aea7cea7100
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Signed-off-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/29849
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Ben Evans <bevans@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
